title: Missed fold: `x == c || (c + 1 <= x && x <= c2)` => `(c <= x && x <= c2)`
summary:
The original test is the idiom:

- `(x == 127) || ((x & -32) == 128)`

This is really a contiguous-range check: `x` is either the single value just below a block boundary (`127`) or in the aligned block starting at the next power-of-2 boundary (`128..159`). The missed optimization was that InstCombine only reasoned about exact `icmp` regions, not that `(x & neg_pow2_mask) == Pow2` represents a whole aligned range.

The patch generalizes this inside `foldAndOrOfICmpsUsingRanges()` by recognizing, on either side, a one-use `and` with a negative power-of-2 mask:
- `X & Mask`, where `Mask` is `m_NegatedPower2`
- paired with constants differing by 1 (`A` and `A+1`)
- where `A+1` is itself a power of 2 and at least `abs(Mask)`

It applies only to the logical forms that encode contiguous ranges:
- `or` of `eq` comparisons
- `and` of `ne` comparisons

When matched, the masked compare is converted from an exact singleton region into `ConstantRange(C, C - Mask)`, i.e. the whole aligned interval, then combined with the other compare via the existing range-folding machinery.

The dev tests broaden coverage beyond the motivating case by checking:
- operand commutation
- both positive and negative forms (`or/eq`, `and/ne`)
- exact-boundary cases where the masked range equals the plain compare
- non-applicable masks/non-power-of-2 constants
- disjoint ranges
- multi-use `and` blocking the transform (`m_OneUse`)
- interactions with other boolean producers (`select`)
- unrelated range-folding cases and multiple masked compares.
